headline: Assessing Applied Materials (AMAT) Valuation After Record Results And Upgraded AI Infrastructure Outlook
summary: Applied Materials (AMAT) has given investors a fresh data point to assess after reporting record quarterly results that beat Wall Street expectations, lifting its outlook for the semiconductor equipment business and widening its role in AI focused chip manufacturing. See our latest analysis for Applied Materials. Those record earnings and new AI focused partnerships have come alongside sharp share price momentum, with a 24.13% 3 month share price return, a 63.86% year to date share price...

headline: Applied Materials Slips Despite Record Quarter and Strong Outlook (AMAT)
summary: Applied Materials (NASDAQ:AMAT) reported record fiscal second-quarter revenue on Thursday and issued third-quarter guidance ahead of Wall Street expectations, supported by continued heavy investment in artificial intelligence infrastructure and data centers that is driving demand for semiconductor manufacturing equipment. AI Infrastructure Spending Continues to Boost DemandThe Santa Clara-based chip equipment maker said it expects current-quarter revenue of approximately $8.

summary: Applied Materials, Inc. (NASDAQ:AMAT) shares fell in premarket trading Friday after the chip equipment maker issued strong third-quarter guidance despite reporting better-than-expected second-quarter results driven by AI-related semiconductor demand. headline: Applied Materials AMAT Q2 2026 Earnings Transcript
summary: Gary Dickerson: Thank you, Mike.  In our second fiscal quarter, Applied Materials delivered record revenue and earnings, along with our highest gross margin in more than 25 years.  With rising demand and increasing long-term visibility from customers, we see an exceptionally strong foundation for sustained multiyear revenue and profit growth.

content.title: Applied Materials AMAT Q2 2026 Earnings Transcript
content.summary: Gary Dickerson: Thank you, Mike.  In our second fiscal quarter, Applied Materials delivered record revenue and earnings, along with our highest gross margin in more than 25 years.  With rising demand and increasing long-term visibility from customers, we see an exceptionally strong foundation for sustained multiyear revenue and profit growth.

headline: These Stocks Are Today’s Movers: Intel, Boeing, Coinbase, Coherent, Microsoft, Cerebras, Figma, Ford, Applied Materials, and More
summary: Stocks were falling Friday, pulling back from record levels, amid concerns over rising Treasury yields and the summit between President Donald Trump and China’s Xi Jinping ending without any clear messaging of what is to come next.  The inference chip maker closed the session with a market capitalization of $66.95 billion, well above the valuation expected before its initial public offering priced on Wednesday.  Investors may have been taking the opportunity to lock in some profit: Heading into Friday’s session, shares were up 71% in 2026.

headline: These Stocks Are Today’s Movers: Intel, Boeing, Cerebras, Microsoft, DexCom, Figma, Coherent, Coinbase, and More
summary: Stocks were falling Friday, pulling back from record levels, as oil prices were rising amid concerns over rising Treasury yields and the summit between President Donald Trump and China’s Xi Jinping coming to an end without any clear messaging of what is next and no meaningful conclusion on the opening of the Strait of Hormuz.  The inference chip maker closed the session with a market capitalization of $66.95 billion, well above the valuation expected before its initial public offering priced on Wednesday.  Investors may have been taking the opportunity to lock in some profit: Heading into Friday’s session, shares were up 71% in 2026.

content.summary: Stocks were falling Friday, pulling back from record levels, as oil prices were rising amid concerns over rising Treasury yields and the summit between President Donald Trump and China’s Xi Jinping coming to an end without any clear messaging of what is next and no meaningful conclusion on the opening of the Strait of Hormuz.  The inference chip maker closed the session with a market capitalization of $66.95 billion, well above the valuation expected before its initial public offering priced on Wednesday.  Investors may have been taking the opportunity to lock in some profit: Heading into Friday’s session, shares were up 71% in 2026.
